entities.name: GENDER PERSPECTIVES
entities.description: Perspectives regarding gender roles and impacts in the context of water security

entities.name: WATER SECURITY
entities.description: The reliable access to an acceptable quantity and quality of water for health, livelihoods, ecosystems, and production

entities.name: INTEGRATED WATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An approach to water management that coordinates the development and management of water, land, and related resources

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The science or practice of farming, including crop cultivation

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Domesticated animals raised in an agricultural setting to provide food, raw materials, and power

entities.name: WATERSHED PROJECT
entities.description: Interventions aimed at managing water resources within a watershed to improve resilience and productivity

entities.name: DROUGHT RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a farming system to withstand and recover from drought conditions

entities.name: GROUNDWATER RECHARGE
entities.description: The process where water moves downward from surface water to groundwater

entities.name: AGROFORESTRY
entities.description: Land use management system in which trees or shrubs are grown around or among crops or pastureland

entities.name: BUNDELKHAND REGION
entities.description: A water-scarce region in Uttar Pradesh, central India

entities.name: UTTAR PRADESH
entities.description: A state in northern India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country in South Asia

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE SURVEY
entities.description: A research method used to collect numerical data from a predefined population

entities.name: QUALITATIVE GENDER AND SOCIAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: Tools used to understand social norms and gender-based benefits and constraints

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit area

entities.name: AGRICULTURE DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: The practice of growing a wider variety of crops to reduce risk and improve livelihoods

entities.name: LIVELIHOOD DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of diversifying income sources to improve economic resilience

entities.name: PATRIARCHAL NORMS
entities.description: Social systems where men hold primary power and predominate in roles of political leadership and social privilege

entities.name: CHECK DAMS
entities.description: Small dams constructed across a valley to slow down water flow and reduce erosion

entities.name: GROUNDWATER LEVEL
entities.description: The depth below the land surface at which the soil or rocks are saturated with water

entities.name: FEMALE EDUCATION
entities.description: The level of schooling and knowledge attained by women

entities.name: REGRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical process for estimating the relationships between a dependent variable and one or more independent variables

entities.name: GENDER INEQUALITY
entities.description: The social process by which men and women are not treated equally

entities.name: BEHAVIORAL CHANGE COMMUNICATION
entities.description: A strategic approach to promote positive health or social behaviors

entities.name: INSTITUTION BUILDING
entities.description: The process of creating or strengthening organizations to manage resources

entities.name: SOCIAL ENGAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of interacting with the community to achieve a goal

entities.name: CAPACITY DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of developing and strengthening the skills and resources that communities need to thrive
